intro: Converting Java to JavaScript trades a rigid, statically typed server environment for a highly dynamic, event-driven runtime environment native to the web ecosystem. This transition is usually driven by full-stack normalization, allowing backend systems to deploy on Node.js engines with minimal architectural footprints. The main adjustment involves adapting class-based object models to single-threaded asynchronous patterns.

diffs.from: Statically checked, strict nominal types requiring full structural matching at compilation phase.
diffs.to: Dynamically typed runtime environment with loose casting protocols and runtime duck-typing validation.

diffs.from: Extremely structural, class-mandatory layouts where everything resides in formal packages and static declarations.
diffs.to: Highly flexible script structure permitting functional paradigms, standalone files, closures, and dynamic object literals.

diffs.from: Predictable, robust JVM garbage collection utilizing memory tuning arguments and generation division.
diffs.to: Automatic engine-level (V8/Spidermonkey) garbage collection leveraging mark-and-sweep routines optimized for short-lived UI scopes.

diffs.from: Multi-threaded architectures running concurrent operations parallelized directly over underlying CPU tasks.
diffs.to: Single-threaded execution queue driving processing pipelines asynchronously via an event-driven loop mechanics.

diffs.from: Massive collection of standardized security, enterprise, IO, and database wrappers tightly maintained by a single governing entity.
diffs.to: Lean native API primarily managing basic types, math, arrays, and JSON operations, relying heavily on third-party registries.

examples.title: Data Object Definition
examples.description: Translating heavy boilerplate POJOs into fast JavaScript object structures.

examples.title: Asynchronous Operations
examples.description: Converting Future-based async methods into native JS Promise resolutions.

tips.title: Watch Variable Scopes
tips.body: Enforce variable scopes cleanly by avoiding 'var' entirely. Rely exclusively on 'const' and 'let' to mirror block behavior.

tips.title: Handle Implicit Coercion
tips.body: Avoid unexpected dynamic evaluations. Use strict comparison expressions ('===') instead of standard double equality symbols ('==').

tips.title: Embrace Dynamic Literals
tips.body: Don't build complex factories or singletons for simple payloads. Instead, leverage simple inline JavaScript object notations directly.

tips.title: Account for Threading Differences
tips.body: Ensure shared multi-threaded cache solutions are reworked into cluster architectures or centralized caches like Redis since JS executes single-threaded.

faqs.question: How do interfaces translate to JavaScript?
faqs.answer: JavaScript doesn't have an equivalent compilation concept for interfaces. Validation is achieved using manual runtime property checking or duck typing.

faqs.question: Can I keep my Java folder structure intact?
faqs.answer: You can, but it is deeply non-idiomatic. JavaScript structures use clean, flat file paths and export statements instead of rigid package hierarchies.

faqs.question: What happens if a property is missing?
faqs.answer: Instead of throwing a reflection exception, JavaScript returns 'undefined'. You must guard against this using optional chaining (?.).
